answer.text: Government-issued currency not backed by a physical commodity like gold
Currency backed by gold reserves held by the central bank
Digital currency issued by a private institution
Currency whose value is determined by a fixed peg to another currency

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Fiat money derives its value from government decree (fiat) and the stability of the issuing government, not from any commodity backing. All modern currencies including USD and INR are fiat currencies. **The Trap:** Gold-backed currency was the gold standard, which ended when the US abandoned it in 1971-73. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII covers the concept of money and currency evolution. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Since fiat currencies aren't commodity-backed, their value depends on monetary policy, economic health, and market confidence.
